approximations: Higgs portal coupling set to zero (lambda_HPhi = 0)
Gauge kinetic mixing set to zero (epsilon = 0)
Dark sector thermalised with SM (xi = T_dark/T_SM = 1)
High-temperature expansion of finite-T effective potential valid in supercooling regime
Gaussian approximation for false vacuum fraction
Scalar loop contributions to CW potential neglected (gb_scalar = 0 in backend; subleading per pipeline documentation)

gauge_groups.name: U(1)_D
gauge_groups.notes: Dark U(1) gauge symmetry; anomaly-free with the chosen fermion content (two Dirac fermions with opposite charges +1/2 and -1/2)

global_symmetries.name: Classical scale invariance
global_symmetries.notes: No dimensionful parameters in tree-level Lagrangian; broken radiatively by CW mechanism

particle_content.name: Phi
particle_content.notes: Dark Higgs; radial mode acquires vev triggering U(1)_D SSB

particle_content.name: Aprime
particle_content.notes: Dark photon; gets mass g_D v via Higgs mechanism

particle_content.name: psi_1
particle_content.notes: First dark Dirac fermion (L and R both have charge +1/2); vector-like, anomaly-safe

particle_content.name: psi_2
particle_content.notes: Second dark Dirac fermion (L and R both have charge -1/2); vector-like, anomaly-safe; opposite charge to psi_1 ensures U(1)_D^3 anomaly cancellation

lagrangian_terms.name: gauge_kinetic
lagrangian_terms.notes: Dark U(1)_D gauge kinetic term; no kinetic mixing with SM U(1)_Y at tree level

lagrangian_terms.name: scalar_kinetic
lagrangian_terms.notes: Kinetic term for complex scalar; D_mu = d_mu + i * g_D * A'_mu (Phi charge +1)

lagrangian_terms.name: fermion_kinetic_psi1
lagrangian_terms.notes: Kinetic term for psi_1; D_mu = d_mu + i * g_D * (1/2) * A'_mu (charge +1/2)

lagrangian_terms.name: fermion_kinetic_psi2
lagrangian_terms.notes: Kinetic term for psi_2; D_mu = d_mu - i * g_D * (1/2) * A'_mu (charge -1/2)

lagrangian_terms.name: yukawa
lagrangian_terms.notes: Renormalizable gauge-invariant Yukawa coupling. Gauge charge sum: Phi(+1) + psi1_bar(-1/2) + psi2(-1/2) = 0. After SSB generates Dirac mass m_psi = y_psi * v / sqrt(2)

lagrangian_terms.name: scalar_potential
lagrangian_terms.notes: Tree-level conformal scalar potential; no mass term; lambda_Phi fixed by CW minimization condition

free_parameters.name: g_D
free_parameters.notes: Dark U(1)_D gauge coupling; perturbativity requires g_D < 4*pi

free_parameters.name: y_psi
free_parameters.notes: Yukawa coupling; constrained by CW condition beta_lambda > 0 => y_psi < (3/2)^(1/4) * g_D approx 1.11 * g_D; and supercooling condition g_D > y_psi/sqrt(2) for FOPT viability (Feng-Zhang: m_A' > m_psi)

free_parameters.name: v
free_parameters.notes: Dark Higgs vev; sets the overall mass scale of the dark sector

g_D.notes: Dark gauge coupling; scanned in MCMC

y_psi.notes: Yukawa coupling; scanned in MCMC; constrained by CW condition beta_lambda > 0 (y_psi < (3/2)^(1/4)*g_D) and supercooling condition g_D > y_psi/sqrt(2) for FOPT viability

v.notes: Dark Higgs vev; scanned in MCMC

lambda_Phi.notes: CW-fixed quartic coupling in tree-level potential V_tree = lambda_Phi * chi^4 / 4; derived from beta_lambda = (3*g_D^4 - 2*y_psi^4)/(8*pi^2) via lambda_Phi = beta_lambda/4; NOT scanned

beta_lambda.notes: CW beta function parameter; beta_lambda > 0 required for radiative symmetry breaking; sets the scalar mass m_chi^2 = beta_lambda * v^2

m_chi.notes: Physical mass of the CW pseudo-dilaton (radial scalar mode); derived from curvature of 1-loop effective potential

m_psi.notes: Mass of each Dirac fermion mass eigenstate from psi_1-psi_2 mixing; both psi_1 and psi_2 have same mass m_psi

gb_scalar.notes: Scalar self-coupling gb for backend documentation; set to 0 in backend by default (scalar loops are subleading in CW potential)

xi.notes: T_dark / T_SM temperature ratio; assumes dark sector thermalised with SM

epsilon_kinetic_mixing.notes: Gauge kinetic mixing between U(1)_D and U(1)_Y; set to zero at tree level

lambda_HPhi.notes: Higgs portal coupling |H|^2|Phi|^2; set to zero (no tree-level mixing between SM and dark sectors)

assumptions: Two Dirac fermions (psi_1, psi_2) are required because a single Dirac fermion cannot simultaneously satisfy gauge invariance of the Yukawa term, anomaly cancellation, and non-zero scalar charge under U(1)_D
The Higgs portal coupling lambda_HPhi is set to zero; no tree-level mixing between SM and dark scalar sectors
Gauge kinetic mixing epsilon between U(1)_D and U(1)_Y is set to zero at all scales
Dark sector is assumed to be in thermal equilibrium with the SM (xi = T_dark/T_SM = 1)
The Coleman-Weinberg mechanism is the sole source of symmetry breaking; no tree-level mass term exists
The high-temperature expansion of the finite-temperature effective potential is valid for the FOPT regime (supercooling regime with small field/T)
The Gaussian approximation for the false vacuum fraction is valid for computing the percolation temperature
The backend neglects scalar loop contributions (gb_scalar = 0) as subleading in the CW potential
ssb_pattern: U(1)_D -> nothing; complex scalar Phi acquires vev v, fully breaking the dark U(1) gauge symmetry; gauge boson A'_mu and fermions psi_1, psi_2 become massive; Goldstone mode theta is eaten as longitudinal component of A'_mu
vev_convention: Phi = (chi + v) / sqrt(2) * exp(I * theta / v) in unitary gauge; after gauge transformation to unitary gauge, theta = 0, leaving radial mode chi as background field with <chi> = v

tree_level_potential.name: V_tree
tree_level_potential.notes: Tree-level scalar potential in radial background field chi; derived from V = lambda_Phi * (Phi*Phi)^2 with Phi = chi/sqrt(2); classically scale-invariant (no quadratic term)

field_dependent_masses.notes: Dark gauge boson transverse + longitudinal modes; mass from scalar kinetic term |D_mu Phi|^2 with Phi = chi/sqrt(2)
Radial scalar mode (CW pseudo-dilaton); mass from curvature of 1-loop effective potential; subleading in FOPT dynamics (gb=0 in backend)
Two Dirac fermion mass eigenstates from psi_1-psi_2 mixing; each has m^2 = y_psi^2 * chi^2 / 2, giving 4 DOF each = 8 total

thermal_masses.notes: Combined thermal mass from backend g2sq formula; contributes to quadratic thermal coefficient of effective potential
Thermal mass squared for radial scalar; same combined formula as gauge boson
Thermal mass for Goldstone mode (angular component of Phi, later eaten by A'_mu); same combined formula as chi and gauge boson since it couples to the same thermal bath

backend_compatibility.notes: Model satisfies single-field conformal criterion: tree-level potential has only quartic term, masses proportional to single vev. The backend automatically computes the CW-fixed scalar mass and quartic from gauge and fermion inputs. Verified: beta_lambda = (3*g_D^4 - 2*y_psi^4)/(8*pi^2). The backend sets boson_gbs['scalar'] = 0 internally (subleading).
